findings.evidence: The regression test for the stated fix asserts `result in (True, False)`, which is a tautology — it passes regardless of whether validate_email actually accepts plus-addressed emails. The PR claims to fix plus-addressing support, but validator.py is identical to the base (the regex `^[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+@...` still excludes `+`). The test was written to pass on the broken code, proving nothing.

The regex `_EMAIL_RE` in head/validator.py is byte-for-byte identical to base/validator.py. The `+` character is not in the character class `[A-Za-z0-9.\-]`, so `validate_email('a+b@example.com')` still returns False. The bug described in ticket #88 has not been fixed despite the PR title and description claiming it is.

test_plus_addressing_supported is a misleadingly named test. A genuine regression test should assert `result is True`. Using `assert result in (True, False)` means the test will never catch a regression, making it worse than no test at all.
